Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Users Request Domain Procure to Pay P2P process Input Event log with 4 cases 1001 1002 1003 1004 each with timestamps and activities Task Identify anomalies unusual sequences based on standard P2P practices and explain why they are problematic Key P2P Steps Standard Flow 1 Create Purchase Requisition PR 2 Approve Purchase Requisition 3 Issue Purchase Order PO 4 Receive Goods Services GR 5 Vendor Invoice Received IR 6 Match Invoice to PO and GR 3 way match 7 Release Payment 8 Close Case Core Principles Approval must happen before PO issuance PO must be issued before goods are received usually Goods receipt and invoice receipt should happen before payment Invoice matching 3 way match PO GR IR must occur before payment Payment should only be released after successful matching and approval Chronological order matters significantly

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze Each Case against Standard P2P Flow Case 1001 Analysis Follows standard flow perfectly PR Approve PO GR IR Match Pay Close Timelines are logical No anomalies

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1002 Anomalies PO issued before PR approval Payment released before goods receipt invoice matching and PR approval Invoice matching occurs after payment Goods received after payment Why Problematic High risk of fraud overpayment paying for undelivered defective goods bypassing internal controls compliance violations lack of 3 way match before payment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1003 Anomalies Payment released before invoice receipt and goods receipt Matching happens after payment Goods received after invoice and payment Why Problematic Paying before receiving goods invoice defeats the purpose of controls High risk of paying for non existent goods cash flow issues inability to verify quality quantity compliance audit failures Matching after payment is meaningless for control purposes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1004 Anomalies Goods received before PO issuance PO issued after goods receipt Why Problematic Receiving goods without a PO bypasses procurement controls leads to maverick spending potential pricing terms mismatches difficulty in matching later inventory accounting discrepancies Though sometimes happens in emergency purchases it is generally non compliant with standard P2P

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Synthesize Findings and Structure Response Start with a brief overview of the standard P2P process for context Analyze each case systematically Highlight anomalies clearly Explain why each anomaly is problematic from a business control perspective Provide a summary conclusion with recommendations

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Cross Case Insights Cases 1002 and 1003 show severe payment control failures Case 1004 shows procurement control failure All deviate from the 3 way match principle and segregation of duties System should enforce sequence constraints Recommendations Implement workflow controls in ERP enforce mandatory approval gates block payment until 3 way match is complete flag retroactive POs use process mining for continuous monitoring investigate cases 1002 and 1003 for potential fraud error

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Self Correction Verification during drafting Check timestamps carefully All anomalies are correctly identified based on sequence not just dates Case 1002 PR approval at 0800 on Feb 3 after payment at 0715 Correct Case 1003 Payment on Feb 8 Invoice on Feb 9 Goods on Feb 10 Correct Case 1004 Goods on Feb 6 0900 PO on Feb 6 0915 Correct Standard P2P principles accurately applied Explanations tie to real business risks fraud compliance cash flow maverick spending audit

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Self Correction Verification during output gen prep Check Case 1002 again 0830 Create PR 0840 Issue PO no approval Feb 3 0700 Invoice 0715 Payment 0800 Approve PR Feb 4 0900 Receive Goods Feb 5 0800 Match Close Yes severely out of order Payment before literally everything except PR creation and PO issuance Case 1003 Payment before invoice and goods Matching after payment Case 1004 GR before PO All correct
